reasoning: Early Q1 2025 reports consistently indicated that South Korea and Vietnam were in the "final stages" of negotiating a $276-$300 million agreement for roughly 20 K9 self-propelled howitzers. While some defense blogs in March loosely claimed a contract was already "signed" or "selected", more authoritative mid-April reports from South Korean media (such as The Korea Herald on April 16) indicated the nations were still "reportedly in negotiations". This confirms the formal government announcement hasn't been finalized yet. However, the explicit high-level focus on defense exports during South Korean Foreign Minister Cho Tae-yul's mid-April visit to Hanoi signals strong political will to finalize the deal soon. Given the advanced stage of negotiations and intense diplomatic engagement, an announcement is substantially more likely than a standard baseline over a two-month timeframe, though typical procurement delays and bureaucratic hurdles in securing unprecedented arms sales slightly temper confidence.
